title: Puerto Banus Catamaran Hire Group Guide 2026: Costs & Tips
html:
For a group holiday in Puerto Banus, hiring a catamaran offers a spacious and sociable way to explore the Costa del Sol. Departing from Puerto Banus marina, a full-day charter for up to 12 guests typically ranges from €1,500 to €3,000 depending on the vessel and season. BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare verified listings and book a charter online.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran for a group in Puerto Banus?

Group catamaran hire in Puerto Banus typically costs between €1,500 and €4,000 per day for a full-day charter. The price varies with boat size, included services, and the time of year. Most charters include a skipper, fuel, and basic amenities; food and drinks are often extra or can be catered. To secure the best rate for your group, compare options on BoatHire24 and book a charter online. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus is the main departure point, a glamorous marina in Marbella on the Costa del Sol. Nearby, the smaller Marbella marina offers an alternative starting point. Across the bay, the port of Estepona provides a quieter base for southern excursions. The Benalmádena marina, a few nautical miles east, is another popular hub for catamaran charters heading toward the scenic Cala de Mijas. For a longer voyage, vessels can set course for the exclusive island of Alboran or the rugged coves of the Costa del Sol coastline. One option worth a look is the Shangani for hire.

Which boat to rent for your group in Puerto Banus

  1. Catamaran, Best for large groups seeking stability and deck space; accommodates 10, 18 guests; from €2,000 for a half day.
  2. Motor Yacht, Ideal for speed and luxury; suitable for 8, 12 guests; from €1,500 for a half day. Models like Azimut and Sunseeker are common.
  3. Sailboat, Perfect for a classic sailing experience; fits 6, 8 guests; from €800 for a half day.
  4. RIB or Tender, Great for watersports and exploring shallow coves; carries 6, 10 guests; from €500 for a half day. Axopar models are popular.
  5. Gulet, Offers traditional wooden charm with ample deck lounging; accommodates 12, 18 guests; from €3,000 for a full day. For a premium option, consider the Shangani for hire.

Puerto Banus catamaran hire costs breakdown

  • Shared group tour (per person): €80, €150 for a half-day trip.
  • Small motorboat (half-day private): €500, €1,000 for a 6, 8 person boat.
  • Catamaran full-day private (12, 18 guests): €2,000, €4,000 including skipper and fuel.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (8, 12 guests): €3,000, €6,000 with full crew and amenities.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

In Spain, a boating licence is generally required to drive a rental boat without a skipper, but most catamaran charters include a licensed skipper. The exact requirements depend on boat power and your nationality; the operator on BoatHire24 will confirm what is needed for your booking. For licence-free enjoyment, opt for a skippered charter, which is the norm for group catamaran hire. The high season runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind often picks up in the afternoon. Check the booking FAQ for detailed guidance on licence rules and booking steps. You can also see more at check availability.

"When chartering a catamaran in Puerto Banus, book a morning departure to enjoy calm seas before the afternoon Poniente wind builds, and always confirm the included fuel range for your planned route."

At a glance

  • Price band: €1,500, €4,000 per full day for a group catamaran
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: half day (4 hours) or full day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 12, 18 guests on most catamarans
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a catamaran without a skipper in Puerto Banus?

Most catamaran charters in Puerto Banus require a licensed skipper, but bareboat hire may be available for experienced sailors with the appropriate licence; check with the listing operator.

What is included in the catamaran hire price?

Standard inclusions are the boat, skipper, fuel, safety equipment, and basic insurance; extras like food, drinks, and water toys are often available for an additional fee.

How far can we sail from Puerto Banus in a day?

A typical day charter can cover 15, 25 nautical miles, allowing visits to nearby coves such as Cala de la Caña or a run along the coast toward Estepona.

What is the cancellation policy for catamaran hires?

Cancellation policies vary by listing; always review the terms on the booking page before confirming, and consider travel insurance for peace of mind.

Whether you are planning a birthday celebration or a corporate outing, Puerto Banus offers an exceptional setting for group catamaran hire. Deck space, sunbathing nets, and a dedicated crew make for a memorable day on the water. To secure your preferred date in 2026, check availability on BoatHire24 and list your boat if you are a boat owner looking to earn from your vessel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
